comment.body: In a two-dimensional system, the centroid is the weighted average. I guess Alma somehow nets out the speed profile and simply takes the weighted average of the skew, that is where different tenors and vanna can play a role. I’m not sure how OD works, but you can simplify by taking the 25-delta put gamma and the 25-delta call gamma and finding their weighted average. But my point is: don’t use formulas you don’t know. Use tools you do know
comment.raw_body: In a two-dimensional system, the centroid is the weighted average. I guess Alma somehow nets out the speed profile and simply takes the weighted average of the skew, that is where different tenors and vanna can play a role. I’m not sure how OD works, but you can simplify by taking the 25-delta put gamma and the 25-delta call gamma and finding their weighted average. But my point is: don’t use formulas you don’t know. Use tools you do know

comment.body: I think the idea behind the centroid is to find the point where net negative and positive gamma forces balance. That gives you the mean of the distribution.
